strategic_capability_mapping.relationship: Moves verifier requirements and recovery repair proposal cardinality into Rust-owned task.run gates rather than accepting LLM-requested checks or dry-run workspace.write proposals as completion evidence.
Preserves proposal.apply as the only mutation path and keeps workspace.write in recovery tasks as a non-mutating repair proposal source that must pass exact-proposal gating.

Records bounded gate status, failure reason, requirement fingerprint, verifier IDs, and proposal counts without raw output, commands, prompts, provider responses, file content, paths, or environment values.

Lets unattended callers distinguish completed repair handoff, missing repair proposal, ambiguous proposals, invalid provenance, and runtime-required verifier retry failure from bounded task.run results.

questions.question: Which strategic capabilities does this phase advance?
What new user-visible, operator-visible, or runtime capability does it add?
Why can existing functionality not substitute for this work?
What concrete product gap remains if this is not implemented?
Is this semantically distinct from the previous three phases?
Can this be done without adding a new RPC?
Can this be done without adding a new summary surface?
What runtime or development-control behavior actually changes?
How does this help headless autonomous development?
How does this move the milestone toward completion?

answers.strategic_capabilities: Advances runtime_permission_enforcement, controlled_workspace_tools, persistent_structured_ledger, and headless_autonomous_development.
answers.new_capability: Recovery task.run now fails closed with bounded verification_recovery_repair gate metadata unless exactly one valid recovery-scoped repair proposal exists, and recovery retry verifier evidence is bound to a runtime-owned requirement fingerprint derived from apply provenance.
answers.why_existing_functionality_is_insufficie: M8.2 returned no repair outcome when workspace.write was denied, allowing a recovery run to complete without a repair proposal, and M8.3 verifier completion gates did not expose runtime-owned requirement identity or apply-bound evidence metadata.
answers.product_gap_if_skipped: Headless callers could confuse a dry-run workspace.write proposal or missing repair proposal with recovery progress and could not audit which runtime-required verifier evidence belonged to the applied repair.
answers.non_duplicative: Yes. R3.1 corrected verifier safety metadata and timeout behavior; M8.1 admitted recovery tasks, M8.2 created repair proposals, and M8.3 retried verification. R3.2 changes the completion gates around those flows.
answers.no_new_rpc: Yes. The implementation extends existing task.run result metadata and terminal ledger payloads only.
answers.no_new_summary: Yes. No report, digest, history, readiness wrapper, or inspection-only endpoint is added.
answers.runtime_behavior_change: Recovery task.run terminal status becomes Failed on missing, ambiguous, or invalid repair proposals, and recovery retry verifier events carry a runtime requirement fingerprint that the completion gate requires.
answers.headless_autonomous_contribution: Callers receive deterministic next_action and failure_reason values from the bounded task.run result, so they can retry, inspect, or escalate without reading raw logs or prompts.
answers.milestone_progress: This repairs the second R3 blocker and leaves diagnostic leakage and replay edge cases as follow-up candidates rather than allowing unsafe recovery completion.

guard_engine_change_review.review_intent: R3.2 updates the current phase manifest and archives the R3.2 manifest. Review must confirm the manifest describes actual task.run gate behavior and does not weaken generic diagnostics or phase-value enforcement.

exit_criteria: verification recovery task.run with denied workspace.write records TaskFailed and returns verification_recovery_repair.gate_status=Failed
missing repair proposal failure uses failure_reason=MissingRecoveryRepairProposal and next_action=inspect_recovery_repair_gate_failure
unusable recovery-scoped proposals with validation_status other than Valid fail with failure_reason=RecoveryRepairProposalNotApplicable
failed repair-gate attempts do not permanently replay-lock the source failure fingerprint; a later authorized recovery start can create a fresh recovery task
successful recovery repair handoff still returns exactly one proposal_id with gate_status=Passed and apply_enabled=false
verification recovery retry task.run emits requirement_source_kind=verification_recovery_retry_apply, source_apply_id, and sha256 requirement_fingerprint on the completion gate
recovery retry controlled verifier terminal evidence carries the same verification_requirement_fingerprint required by the gate
workspace.write proposal generation remains non-mutating and proposal.apply remains the only workspace mutation path
VSIX protocol validation accepts bounded passed and failed repair gate outcomes and rejects raw fields
no new RPC, report, digest, history, readiness wrapper, or inspection surface is added

test_tokens: accepts bounded task.run verification recovery repair outcomes and rejects raw fields

next_phase_after_r3_2: R3.3 Runtime Recovery Diagnostics Leak Repair
